project.title: SChLAP1 pulldown, Hargrove Lab Duke University
project.projectDescription: Test v Control IP of SChLAP1 analyzed by bottoms-up proteomics approach on Orbitrap Astral.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Samples were spiked with 1 pmol bovine casein as an internal quality control standard and then brought to 5% SDS, reduced for 15 min at 80C with 10 mM dithiolthreitol, and alkylated with 20 mM iodoacetamide for 30 min at room temperature. Samples were then supplemented with a final concentration of 1.2% phosphoric acid and 375 ÂµL of S-Trap (Protifi) binding buffer (90% MeOH/100mM TEAB). Proteins were trapped on the S-Trap micro cartridge, digested using 20 ng/ÂµL sequencing grade trypsin (Promega) for 1 hr at 47C, and eluted using 50 mM TEAB, followed by 0.2% FA, and lastly using 50% ACN/0.2% FA. All samples were then lyophilized to dryness. Samples were resuspended in 40uL of 1% TFA/2% acetonitrile with 12.5 fmol/ÂµL of yeast ADH.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: LC/MS/MS was performed using an EvoSep One UPLC coupled to a Thermo Orbitrap Astral high resolution accurate mass tandem mass spectrometer (Thermo). Briefly, each sample loaded EvoTip was eluted onto a 1.5 Âµm EvoSep 150um ID x 15cm performance (EvoSep) column using the SPD30 gradient at 55C.  Data collection on the Orbitrap Astral mass spectrometer was performed in a data-independent acquisition (DIA) mode of acquisition with a r=240,000 (@ m/z 200) full MS scan from m/z 380-980 in the OT with a target AGC value of 4e5 ions. Fixed DIA windows of 4 m/z from m/z 380 to 980 DIA MS/MS scans were acquired in the Astral with a target AGC value of 5e4 and max fill time of 6 ms. HCD collision energy setting of 27% was used for all MS2 scans. Data were imported into Spectronaut 19 (Biognosis). Relative peptide abundance were measured based on MS2 fragment ions of selected ion chromatograms of the retention time aligned runs. The MS/MS data was searched against a SwissProt Human database (downloaded in 2024), a common contaminant/spiked protein database (bovine albumin, bovine casein, yeast ADH, etc.), and an equal number of reversed-sequence â€œdecoysâ€� for false discovery rate determination. A Direct DIA+ library free approach within Spectonaut was used to perform the database searches. Database search parameters included fixed modification on Cys (carbamidomethyl) with variable modification on Met (oxidation). Full trypsin enzyme rules were used along with 10ppm mass tolerances on precursor ions and 20ppm on product ion. Spectral annotation was set at a maximum 1% peptide false discovery rate based on q-value calculations. Note that peptide homology was addressed using razor rules in which a peptide matched to multiple different proteins was exclusively assigned to the protein has more identified peptides. Protein homology was addressed by grouping proteins that had the same set of peptides to account for their identification. A master protein within a group was assigned based on % coverage.

references.referenceLine: Falese JP, McFadden EJ, D'Inzeo CA, Hargrove AE. Structural analysis of the lncRNA SChLAP1 reveals protein binding interfaces and a conformationally heterogenous retroviral insertion. RNA. 2025 31(9):1260-1286
